tagline: Bangkok calm with a city pulse
footerBlurb: A warm, design-led city stay built for long weekends, quick business trips, and repeat guests who care about neighborhood energy as much as the room.

seoTitle: Awtan Sukhumvit Hotel | Boutique Bangkok Stay
seoDescription: A Sanity-managed boutique hotel homepage with story-driven sections, offers, room teasers, facilities, reviews, and neighborhood contact details.

sections.title: A warm city base shaped for short stays, long lunches, and easy nights out.
sections.description: Awtan blends calm guestrooms, soft service, and a walkable Sukhumvit address into a homepage structure now sourced from Sanity.
sections.bookingCardText: Use this panel for booking engine links, best-rate messaging, rewards, or campaign CTAs.

sections.title: A neighborhood-first stay with the pace of Sukhumvit just outside the lobby.
sections.description: This section carries the hotel story, positioning, and a few proof points while the marketing team replaces the seeded starter copy in Studio.
highlights: Walkable to BTS and neighborhood dining
Quiet, layered interiors instead of generic business-hotel finishes
Flexible for both short business trips and weekend city breaks

sections.title: Room types that sell the stay before the full rooms page exists.
sections.description: Homepage room teasers should stand on their own now and still point naturally to a future /rooms route later.

rooms.title: Deluxe King
rooms.summary: A calm base with a lounge chair, city-facing windows, and enough space to settle in.

rooms.title: Corner Twin
rooms.summary: Built for friends and flexible travel with extra daylight and a relaxed living feel.

sections.title: Small but considered facilities that matter to real guests.
sections.description: Use this module to highlight the features that make the property feel complete without turning the page into a generic checklist.

amenities.title: Rooftop dip pool
amenities.description: A compact top-floor pool for late afternoon resets and skyline views.

amenities.title: All-day cafe
amenities.description: Coffee, breakfast, and easy lunches designed for guests and neighborhood walk-ins.

amenities.title: Gym and recovery corner
amenities.description: Cardio, weights, and a small stretch zone for guests staying in motion.

sections.title: Campaign space for rewards, long-stay value, and seasonal booking pushes.
sections.description: These cards mirror the promotional rhythm of the reference site and give marketing room to rotate value props.

offers.title: Book direct and unlock late check-out
offers.summary: A simple reward-driven offer block for direct-booking positioning.

offers.title: Weekend stay with breakfast for two
offers.summary: Bundle breakfast, flexible arrival, and a neighborhood guide for short leisure stays.

sections.title: Reviews should feel like reassurance, not filler.
sections.description: A review band helps the homepage feel commercially complete before deeper page inventory exists.

testimonials.name: Mina T.
testimonials.quote: The location made everything easy, but the calm rooms were what made us extend our stay.

testimonials.name: Daniel R.
testimonials.quote: It felt designed for actual travelers, not just for photos. Fast check-in, quiet nights, and great coffee downstairs.

sections.title: Collect interest for launches, offers, and neighborhood updates.
sections.description: This v1 homepage uses a CTA pattern rather than a wired form so marketing can decide the final signup flow later.
sections.helperText: Plug in your email platform or CRM destination when the campaign stack is ready.

sections.title: Close with location confidence and direct booking contact.
sections.description: This final block gives the homepage an operational close: address, hours, contact, and a strong route into booking or inquiry.
